title: TDD Rust broker server, endpoints, config, cancellation lifecycle, and minimal addon registries

do_not_read: other directories in ~/dev unless explicitly referenced

scope: Implement the standalone Rust local broker server and minimal addon system.
goals: Use the tdd skill with vertical slices.
Expose GET /health, POST /v1/autocomplete, POST /v1/cancel/:request_id, and POST /v1/reload.
Validate protocol messages and reject invalid requests.
Route requests to mock or pi provider by config only; clients must not need provider changes.
Apply context/prompt/postprocessor registries inside the broker.
Add config loading with privacy exclusions, remote context byte limit, provider selection, trigger defaults, and language-specific code/markdown prompt selection.
Represent debounce/cancellation semantics in request lifecycle and tests.
non_goals: Do not create cloud auth or remote broker support.
safety: No autocomplete request may implicitly read arbitrary files.
Sensitive path exclusions must be enforced before remote/pi calls.
success_criteria: cargo test passes for server/addon/config slices.
HTTP tests cover health, valid autocomplete, invalid autocomplete, cancel, reload, provider selection, and postprocessing path.
Mock provider can serve VS Code and Obsidian-style requests.
